IIT Madras Latest Updates and News

30 Jun, 2026 IIT Madras JEE Advanced Cutoff 2026 has been released. Check round and course-wise IIT Madras JEE Advanced Cutoff 2026.

  • Round 2 Cutoff for B.Tech is 149-7837 for the General Category.
...Read More

IIT Madras cutoff is available for JEE-Advanced, GATE, IIT-JAM, CAT here. You can check category wise IIT Madras cutoff for popular courses such as B.Tech, BS, B.Tech + M.Tech, BS + MS, MA, M.Tech, MBA, M.Sc below.

IIT Madras offers admission to B.Tech through JEE-Advanced. For Admission in BS courses, IIT Madras accept JEE-Advanced score. You can check the table below to know the accepted exams for courses offered by IIT Madras (Cutoff Range below is across all categories).

Course Exam Cutoff
B.Tech JEE-Advanced 2 - 7837
BS JEE-Advanced 63 - 11166
B.Tech + M.Tech JEE-Advanced 117 - 3175
MA GATE 167 - 737
M.Tech GATE 199 - 944
MBA CAT 85
M.Sc IIT-JAM 157 - 5485

IIT Madras JEE-Advanced Cutoff 2026

Students can take admission in B.Tech, BS, B.Tech + M.Tech, BS + MS programs at IIT Madras based on JEE-Advanced score. As per IIT Madras JEE-Advanced Cutoff 2026, the overall cutoff for B.Tech programs in Round 2 is 2 - 7837 across all categories.

  • For BS, The Overall closing rank in 2026 in Round 2 is 63 - 11166 across all categories.
  • For B.Tech + M.Tech, The Overall closing rank in 2026 in Round 2 is 117 - 3175 across all categories.

B.Tech Computer Science and Engineering, BS Mathematics, B.Tech + M.Tech Engineering Design, are the top choices for students appearing in JEE-Advanced.

In 2026, B.Tech Computer Science and Engineering closing rank for General Category is 149 in Round 2.

  • For BS Mathematics, The JEE-Advanced closing rank 2026 for General is 1470 - 1695 in Round 2.
  • For B.Tech + M.Tech Engineering Design, The JEE-Advanced closing rank 2026 for General is 3078 in Round 2.

You can check B.Tech Computer Science and Engineering, BS Mathematics, B.Tech + M.Tech Engineering Design, category-wise closing rank for JEE-Advanced below:

IIT Madras JEE-Advanced Cutoff Trends

Admission at IIT Madras in B.Tech Programs in 2026 was closed at 7837 rank whereas closing rank in 2025 was 7433 across all categories.

  • Admission in BS Programs in 2026 was closed at 11166 rank whereas closing rank in 2025 was 10290 across all categories.
  • Admission in B.Tech + M.Tech Programs in 2026 was closed at 3175 rank whereas closing rank in 2025 was 3380 across all categories.
  • Admission in BS + MS Programs in 2024 was closed at 2742 rank whereas closing rank in 2023 was 3171 across all categories.

IIT Madras GATE Cutoff 2026

Students can take admission in MA, M.Tech programs at IIT Madras based on GATE score. As per IIT Madras GATE Cutoff 2025, the overall cutoff for MA programs in Round 1 is 167 - 737 across all categories.

  • For M.Tech, The Overall closing marks in 2025 in Round 1 is 199 - 944 across all categories.

MA Development Studies, M.Tech Artificial Intelligence & Data Science, are the top choices for students appearing in GATE.

In 2025, MA Development Studies closing marks for General Category is 679 in Round 1.

  • For M.Tech Artificial Intelligence & Data Science, The GATE closing marks 2025 for General is 866 in Round 1.

Cutoff Summary: IIT Madras

  • GATE
  • JEE Advanced
  • IIT JAM

The GATE 2025 cutoff for admission to IIT Madras in the General category ranges from a score of 944 to 487. The admission cutoff for M.Tech Integrated Circuit Technology is 944, followed by M.Tech Computer Science and Engineering at 881. Other competitive programs such as Microelectronics and VLSI Design, Artificial Intelligence & Data Science, and Electronic System Design have cutoffs in the range of 881 to 829. Less popular courses, including Petroleum Engineering, Nanotechnology, and Clinical Engineering, show admission scores between 487 and 513.

For OBC candidates, the cutoff scores range from 862 for M.Tech Integrated Circuit Technology to 304 for M.Tech Industrial Mathematics & Scientific Computing. In the SC category, the range is 760 for M.Tech Microelectronics and VLSI Design to 167 for MA English. For ST candidates, the admission score is 651 for M.Tech Microelectronics and VLSI Design and 226 for M.Tech Biomedical Engineering. Under the EWS category, the cutoff ranges from 854 in M.Tech Integrated Circuit Technology to 287 in MA English.

...Read More
 By Collegedunia’s Personal AI

Can I get an IIT with 4000 JEE Advanced Rank?

Harish Sharma

4,000 JEE Advanced rank is genuinely a solid score — you're in the top ~22% of all qualified candidates and most IIT seats are within reach.

what you can realistically get (general category):

  • Good IITs in non-CSE branches: IIT Roorkee (Chemical, Civil, Mech, Electrical), IIT Guwahati (similar branches), IIT BHU/IIT Dhanbad (ECE, EE, Mech) are realistic
  • IIT Kharagpur, IIT Kanpur: some non-core branches might just open at 4000
  • IIT Delhi: very limited at 4000 general — some interdisciplinary programs (Maths & Computing, Engineering Physics) may open but CSE/ECE/EE won't
  • IIT Bombay, Madras: highly unlikely at 4000 general for any branch
  • Newer IITs (Bhilai, Goa, Dharwad): easy to get CSE/ECE here

one thing people miss — if you're female, the supernumerary seats at all IITs have more relaxed cutoffs than gender-neutral seats, so more doors open. similarly, if you're OBC/SC/ST check your category rank separately — 4000 AIR with a good category rank can get you significantly better options.

...Read More
0
0

I scored 121 in my JEE exam. I would like to know if I’m eligible for admission to IIT Madras.

Muskan Agrahari

No, with 121 in JEE Main, you will not be eligible for IIT Madras admission because IITs accept JEE Advanced scores and not JEE Main. If your 121 is JEE Main, check if you are among the first 2.5 lakh students who can appear for JEE Advanced. Only candidates with a good rank in JEE Advanced can get through in IIT Madras.

Quick Breakdown:

Criteria Details
Exam Required JEE Advanced (not JEE Main)
What your score means 121 in JEE Main may not qualify you for JEE Advanced cutoff
Admission Process Qualify JEE Main ? Appear for JEE Advanced ? Get rank within cutoff
Cutoff at IIT Madras (2024) CSE: ~AIR 171 (OPEN), Least competitive: ~AIR 6926
...Read More

What are the selection procedures of an MS in IIT Madras and the GATE cutoff for civil engineering?

Arati Sinhastudied at Indian Institute of Technology, Madras

The selection procedure for MS (Master of Science) at IIT Madras typically involves the following steps:

  1. Online Application: You must apply online through the official website of IIT Madras. They need to fill in personal, academic, and other relevant details in the application form.
  2. Entrance Exam: You have to appear for the entrance exam to get admission to the MS program. For engineering programs, the Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering (GATE) is the most common entrance exam.
  3. Shortlisting: After the entrance exam, you will be shortlisted based on your performance in the exam.
  4. Interview: You will be called in for an interview. The interview assesses your knowledge in respective fields, problem-solving skills, and communication skills.
  5. Final Selection: The final selection will be based on your performance in the entrance exam, interview, and academic records.

GATE cutoff for civil engineering at IIT Madras varies every year and depends on various factors such as the number of applicants, the difficulty level of the exam, and the number of available seats. 

The GATE cutoff for IIT Madras civil engineering for the last three years is given below:

Category

GATE 2021

GATE 2020

GATE 2019

General

27

28.2

28.2

OBC-NCL

24.3

25.4

25.4

SC/ST/PwD

18.9

18.8

18.8

It's important to note that the cutoff scores can vary from year to year and you should aim to score well above the cutoff to increase their chances of getting admission to IIT Madras for an MS in civil engineering.

What AIR should I get to get into IIT Madras?

Anamika Sinhastudied at Indian Institute of Technology, Madras

To get into IIT Madras, you need to clear JEE Advanced exam. Your All India Rank (AIR) in JEE Advanced is crucial for admission to IIT Madras, as the institute considers only candidates who clear the JEE Advanced exam and meet the institute's cutoff criteria.

The minimum AIR required for admission to IIT Madras varies from year to year and depends on several factors such as the number of applicants, the difficulty level of the exam, and the number of available seats. In general, you should aim to secure an AIR in the top 500-1000 to have a good chance of getting admission to IIT Madras.

To achieve this rank, you need to prepare well for the JEE Advanced exam, which tests your understanding of concepts in physics, chemistry, and mathematics. You should practice solving problems and take mock tests to improve your speed and accuracy.

Apart from your AIR, IIT Madras also considers other factors such as your performance in Class 12 exams, your academic background, and your performance in other co-curricular activities. So, focus on your overall development and maintain a good academic record to increase your chances of getting admission to IIT Madras.

What is the CAT cut-off for DOMS IIT Madras?

Harleen SethiStudied at the Indian Institute of Technology, Madras (IITM)

The cut-off score for CAT for the Department of Management Studies (DOMS) at IIT Madras varies from year to year and is subject to change. However, for the 2022-2024 batch, the CAT cut off for General category candidates was 95, while the cut-off for OBC category was 85.

It's important to note that the CAT score is only one of the criteria for admission to the MBA program at DOMS IIT Madras. The institute also considers academic performance, work experience, and performance in group discussions and personal interview rounds while making admission decisions.
